chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ يَخْرُجُ مِنْ مَكَّةَ لَا يُرِيدُ الْعَوْدَ إِلَيْهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – The one who goes out from Makkah not intending the return to it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/152)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/152)
text: كَانَ عَلِيٌّ ( صلوات الله عليه ) يَقُولُ لِوُلْدِهِ يَا بَنِيَّ انْظُرُوا بَيْتَ رَبِّكُمْ فَلَا يَخْلُوَنَّ مِنْكُمْ فَلَا تُنَاظَرُوا .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hajjal, from Hammad,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> was saying to his<sup>asws</sup> sons: ‘O my<sup>asws</sup> sons! Consider the House (Kabah) of your Lord<sup>azwj</sup>, so as that it would never be empty from you, and never argue (about it - meaning keep on visiting without having second thoughts on visiting it)’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from al-Hajjal from Hammad who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that ’Amir al-Mu’minin would say to his sons, ‘My sons, take the House of your Lord in your consideration so it is not left without your presence therein; otherwise, you will risk coming of torment upon you.’”
